explanation: The input specifies that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator must not command angular roll accelerations greater than the system capability for more than 10 time units. The atomic proposition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' directly captures when this undesired condition occurs. To formally specify that such a condition does not persist for more than 10 time units, we use the template for 'forbidden state for longer than N time units' (template index 3). This LTL formula enforces that either such a condition is never sustained for >10 time units (by restricting the global occurrence in windows of length 10), and if exceeded, it is only possible in a transient sense and must be left within N+1 units.

This LTL property expresses that it is always the case that the angular roll accelerations are NOT greater than the system's capability for stretches longer than 10 time units. In formal LTL, this means globally, over all possible executions, the system cannot have 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' persist for more than 10 consecutive time units. The chosen template (index 3) fits best, as it requires that for any time period of 10 consecutive units, the condition does not persist throughout, but if it does appear, it must cease within 11 time units (per the template's intent and the property described). This captures the intent of limiting duration of excessive command to 10 time units.

The requirement states that the Inner Loop Roll Regulator must not command angular roll accelerations greater than the system's capability (50 deg/sec^2) for durations longer than 10 time units. This is best represented by saying that it is always not the case that roll accelerations exceeding this threshold persist for more than the allowed duration. Template 3, which states: '(((G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | (G (! bool_exp3))) & (F[0,N_DURATION+1] bool_exp3))', captures that it is never true for a window of N_DURATION=10 that the unsafe (too large) accelerations are continuously commanded (if it were, this would violate the requirement). Here, bool_exp3 is 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability'. This template enforces that any such unsafe command cannot persist for longer than the specified bound.

This LTL property formalizes the requirement that the system must not command angular roll accelerations greater than its capability (50 deg/sec^2) for more than 10 time units. The template uses the globally (always) operator to enforce that, in every time window of length 10 units, it is not possible for the proposition 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' to remain true throughout the window, which would correspond to continuously exceeding capability over 10 units. Additionally, the 'finally' operator after 10 units forces the occurrence of a time point where the acceleration does not exceed the capability within that duration. This precisely captures the intent of limiting the exceeded state to durations less than or equal to 10 time units.

This property ensures that it is never the case that angular roll accelerations commanded by the Inner Loop Roll Regulator exceed the specified capability (i.e., 50deg/sec2) for more than 10 time units. It captures the duration aspect by stating that, in any 11 consecutive time units, 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' is not true at all times; in other words, the accelerations cannot remain above the capability continuously for more than 10 time steps.

The requirement states that the inner loop roll regulator should not command angular roll accelerations greater than the system's capability (50 deg/sec^2) for more than 10 time units. This is best represented by stating that it is always the case that, over any interval of 11 time units (i.e., more than 10), it is not possible that the condition "angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability" holds throughout this interval. That is, the condition cannot be true for longer than 10 time units at once. This can be captured using an LTL template that says it is never the case that the condition is true for more than N_DURATION time units. Therefore, "Globally, for every interval of 11 time units, it cannot be the case that the roll acceleration limit is exceeded throughout."

This LTL property asserts that it is always true (globally) that angular roll accelerations greater than the system capability (50deg/sec2) NEVER persist for intervals longer than 10 time units. This captures the requirement by stating that such excessive accelerations are not allowed for more than the specified duration, reflecting a design constraint that protects the system from over-commanding beyond its physical capabilities.

This LTL property formalizes the requirement that the 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' condition should not hold for durations exceeding 10 time units. By using the template (G[0,N_DURATION] (! bool_exp3)) | (G (! bool_exp3)), we express that for any period of up to 10 time units or in the global case, the system does not command angular roll accelerations greater than 50deg/sec2 for longer than allowed, thus capturing the limit on the duration as specified in the natural language statement.

The requirement states that the system should not command angular roll accelerations greater than its capability (50 deg/sec^2) for more than 10 time units. In temporal logic, this is best captured by stating that it's always the case that periods where angular roll acceleration exceeds the capability must not last longer than 10 time units. The template (3) captures this: either the property never holds over a given window or, if it does occur, it must be followed by at least one time step where it stops within the required time. Here, 'angular_roll_accelerations_greater_than_capability' is used as the main condition, and N_DURATION is set to 10.

This LTL property uses the template for bounded safety: it specifies that for any window of 10 time units (N_DURATION=10), it is always the case that 'angular roll accelerations greater than the capability' does not persist throughout the entire window, i.e., the property can occur but not for more than 10 consecutive time units. The corresponding LTL formula ensures that if this unsafe condition arises, it cannot last longer than the allowed duration, faithfully capturing the requirement from the natural language statement.
